methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a limited comp universe of two private peers (Shinkei and Daylight Computer) with no direct public comparables available in the sector.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised of $40M as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $80M. This single-method result anchors the triangulation analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, with a median of $80M. The range reflects inherent uncertainty around the 2.0x multiplier in a two-company comp set lacking sufficient scale or disclosure depth. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the stage-progression multiplier for this specific company, limited visibility into growth trajectories and burn rates, and sparse public market benchmarks in consumer tech hardware. A broader comp set and additional methods (revenue multiple, DCF sensitivity) would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
